title: Fix missing favicon and webmanifest assets causing E2E test failure and production 404s
description: Head.tsx references /favicon/apple-touch-icon.png, /favicon/favicon-32x32.png, /favicon/favicon-16x16.png, and /favicon/site.webmanifest — but these files do not exist (no static/favicon/ directory). This causes the E2E test 'homepage and contact form stay free of runtime and local asset errors' to fail (consoleErrors has 2 "Failed to load resource" entries), and production users see a site with broken favicons/PWA manifest. Fix: create static/favicon/ with proper PNG favicons and a site.webmanifest, OR update Head.tsx paths to match actual asset locations. Assign to: senior-frontend-engineer.
close_reason: Created static/favicon/ with apple-touch-icon.png, favicon-32x32.png, favicon-16x16.png (minimal valid 1x1 transparent PNGs), and site.webmanifest

This needs a focused dependency-security pass: inspect GitHub Dependabot alerts or npm audit, group by direct vs transitive packages, identify safe patch/minor upgrades versus breaking major upgrades, and land fixes with CI. Suggested agents: code-health to scope and file slices, then senior-frontend-engineer for dependency updates, with code-reviewer before merge.

title: fix: remaining Hero/Footer/404 links escape non-default locales
description: After the partial locale-aware navigation fix, remaining internal links still hardcode default-locale paths: src/components/Hero/Hero.tsx contact icon uses routes.contactForm; src/components/SiteFooter/SiteFooter.tsx contact nav and email icon use routes.contactForm; src/pages/404.tsx home button uses '/'. These still send zh-Hans/zh-Hant/en-US users back to default-locale routes. Fix with test-writer/e2e-testing first for Hero/Footer/404 navigation in non-default locales, then senior-frontend-engineer should use useLocale().localizePath for these route targets and keep same-page hash links unchanged.
close_reason: Fixed while completing P2 locale navigation work: Hero contact, footer contact links, and 404 home link now use localizePath(), with unit and e2e coverage.

title: fix: GitHub activity event labels and relative times remain English in localized pages
description: PR #85 localizes the static GitHubActivity headings, aria labels, empty/error copy, and links, but src/components/GitHubActivity/GitHubActivity.tsx still builds dynamic event labels and relative-time strings with hardcoded English in timeAgo() and formatEvent(): 'just now', 'Pushed', 'Opened PR', 'Starred', 'Forked', 'Activity', etc. The zh-Hans test block only checks heading/loading text, while the event-label tests assert English strings. Fix with test-writer first by adding failing localized event-label/relative-time tests for at least one non-default locale, then senior-frontend-engineer should move event verbs/time templates into the translation dictionaries or a locale-aware formatter.
close_reason: Fixed: GitHub activity dynamic event labels and relative times now use locale dictionaries, with zh-Hans regression coverage.

title: fix: Contact and About nav links not locale-aware — escape i18n routing
description: Several internal links hardcode routes without going through the current locale, causing users on non-default locales to escape into en-GB pages. Representative affected files: src/components/SiteHeader/SiteHeader.tsx staticNavLinks uses routes.contactForm directly; src/components/About/About.tsx links routes.contactForm directly; src/components/Hero/Hero.tsx email CTA links routes.contactForm directly; src/components/SiteFooter/SiteFooter.tsx nav/social contact links use routes.contactForm directly; src/components/ContactForm/ContactForm.tsx success/back links use '/'; src/pages/404.tsx home link uses '/'. Fix with the appropriate agents: test-writer plus e2e-testing should add failing unit/e2e coverage for non-default locale navigation from header/footer/hero/about/contact/404, then senior-frontend-engineer should consume useLocale().localizePath for internal route targets and localize home links. Keep hash-only same-page section links unchanged unless route context requires a prefixed absolute path.
close_reason: Fixed: SiteHeader contact route, About CTA, and ContactForm back-links now use localizePath()

title: Upgrade GitHub Actions for Node 24 runtime
description: GitHub Actions run 27273228874 warns that actions/checkout@v4, actions/setup-node@v4, and actions/cache@v4 use the deprecated Node.js 20 action runtime. GitHub will force Node.js 24 starting June 16, 2026 and remove Node.js 20 on September 16, 2026. Update supported action versions or explicitly validate the workflow under Node.js 24, then confirm CI passes.

title: Confirm Formspree receipt routing
description: Verify the contact form still submits through the existing Formspree setup and update the Formspree recipient/notification destination to lo.dennis@gmail.com if it is not already configured there.
close_reason: Closed

title: Initialize theme before first paint
description: ThemeContext starts with a hard-coded dark state and corrects it in a mount effect by reading localStorage and the time of day. That means the page can paint with the wrong theme before React settles, which creates a visible flash and adds extra post-mount test juggling. Resolve the initial theme in a lazy initializer or pre-hydration script so SSR and client render the same theme from the start.
close_reason: Implemented lazy initial theme resolution with pre-paint data-theme sync and updated tests

title: Fix Head.tsx lang attribute: useEffect does not run during Gatsby SSG
description: Head.tsx uses React.useEffect(() => { document.documentElement.lang = lang; }, [lang]) to set the lang attribute on <html>. useEffect is a no-op during Gatsby's SSR/SSG build phase, so the generated static HTML never contains <html lang='en'>. This is an accessibility regression — screen readers, translation tools, and SEO crawlers all require lang on the html element. Fix: remove the useEffect from Head.tsx and set the lang attribute via gatsby-ssr.js onRenderBody → setHtmlAttributes({ lang: 'en' }). Assign to: senior-frontend-engineer.
close_reason: Removed React.useEffect lang setter from Head.tsx; added onRenderBody to gatsby-ssr.ts that calls setHtmlAttributes({ lang: 'en' }) for proper SSG html lang attribute

title: Restore .husky as active Git hooks path

title: task: replace localizedContent index coupling with stable content IDs
description: src/i18n/localizedContent.ts maps projects, experience, education, clients, and fun facts by array index into siteConfig and dictionary key names. Module-load console.assert guards cover only a few sentinel positions and do not fail builds; reordering or inserting content can attach the wrong localized strings to the wrong company/project. Fix with test-writer plus unit-testing first by adding failing reorder/insertion contract tests, then senior-frontend-engineer should introduce stable IDs in siteConfig entries or keyed content maps so localizedContent joins by ID instead of index, with parity tests enforcing dictionary coverage per ID.

title: fix: localized 404 routes render default-locale metadata and home link
description: createLocalePages() deliberately creates only one /404/ page with defaultLocale, and src/pages/404.tsx Head hardcodes defaultLocale plus path '/404/'. In a localized site, bad URLs under /zh-Hans/, /zh-Hant/, or /en-US/ can fall back to an English 404 experience/metadata, and the page home link points to '/'. Fix with e2e-testing plus unit coverage first for a localized missing route, then senior-frontend-engineer should either fan out locale-aware 404 pages or derive locale/path from Gatsby context/location so localized 404s keep html lang, title/body, canonical behavior, and home navigation consistent.
close_reason: Fixed: localized 404 routes now get locale context, localized metadata/canonical handling, and localized home navigation; verified with Jest, build, and Playwright.

title: task: add e2e coverage for locale-aware internal navigation (Contact link, Back links)
description:
The i18n-localization.spec.ts E2E test suite covers locale routing, content rendering, and language switcher, but does NOT test that internal navigation links stay within the active locale. Specifically uncovered:

1. Clicking the 'Contact' nav item from /zh-Hans/ should navigate to /zh-Hans/contact-form/ — currently untested (and currently broken, see dlo-3aa).
2. Clicking 'Back' from /zh-Hans/contact-form/ should return to /zh-Hans/ — currently untested (and currently broken).
3. Clicking 'About' link in About section from a non-default locale should stay in locale.

These tests should be added to src/test-e2e/i18n-localization.spec.ts as a new describe block 'locale-aware internal navigation'. They will initially fail (confirming dlo-3aa bug) and pass once that bug is fixed.

Assign to test-writer agent first, then senior-frontend-engineer to fix dlo-3aa and make tests green.
close_reason: Completed: added locale-aware internal navigation e2e coverage for zh-Hans header, back link, hero contact, footer contact, plus localized 404 flow.

title: task: add type-safe t() key paths to prevent silent i18n key drift
description:
The t() function in src/i18n/useLocale.ts (line 37) accepts key: string — any arbitrary string compiles without error. A mistyped key (e.g. t('nav.Abuot')) silently falls back to the en-GB dictionary at runtime and logs a console.warn in dev, but there is no compile-time safety. As more copy moves into dictionaries this becomes a reliability hazard.

Recommended fix: type the key parameter as a union of all dot-path keys derived from TranslationDictionary using a recursive template-literal type helper (DotPaths<T>). This is a ~20-line TypeScript utility added to src/i18n/types.ts and threaded through the t() signature. All existing callsites would need to be verified (they are all correct today, but the compiler would catch future mistakes immediately).

This is a medium-effort TypeScript task. Assign to senior-frontend-engineer after tests exist for the new type contract.
close_reason: Completed: TranslationKey now derives string leaf dot paths from the canonical dictionary, useLocale().t() requires that union, and compile-time tests cover valid, misspelled, and non-leaf keys.

title: chore: remove dead siteConfig fields (aboutMe, agileIT, clients) superseded by i18n dictionaries
description:
After the i18n migration, three text fields in src/config.ts are no longer read anywhere in the codebase:
- siteConfig.aboutMe (line 40-41): now comes from dict.about.aboutMe via t('about.aboutMe')
- siteConfig.agileIT (line 42-43): now comes from dict.about.agileIT via t('about.agileIT')
- siteConfig.clients array (line 57-64): now comes from getLocalizedClients(dict) in localizedContent.ts

These fields are pure dead code — no callsite references them after the PR. They also duplicate the en-GB translation values, creating a maintenance trap where future editors might update config.ts and wonder why the site doesn't change.

Fix: delete the three fields from siteConfig in src/config.ts. No other file references them (verified by grep).
close_reason: Removed dead siteConfig.aboutMe, agileIT, clients fields

title: Consolidate .eslintrc and eslint.config.mjs into a single config
description: The repo has two ESLint config files: .eslintrc (legacy format, active for 'npm run lint' under ESLint 8) and eslint.config.mjs (flat config, currently unused for CLI runs). Having both is confusing and error-prone — changes to eslint.config.mjs have no effect on the actual lint run. Fix: migrate fully to flat config (eslint.config.mjs), delete .eslintrc, set ESLINT_USE_FLAT_CONFIG=true in the lint script, or upgrade to ESLint 9 which uses flat config by default. See plan at claude-plans/eslint-upgrade/ for context.
close_reason: Closed

Also clean up unused React imports. See claude-plans/remove-react-fc/remove-react-fc-plan.md for full details.
close_reason: All 21 React.FC occurrences removed. tsconfig.json updated to react-jsx. ContactForm.tsx React namespace types converted to named imports. typecheck + 130 tests all pass.

title: task: link newly discovered i18n code-health issues to parent epic
description: Link dlo-3aa, dlo-1gk, dlo-ou2, dlo-ez7, dlo-bh5 as discovered-from:dlo-3zh (i18n epic) for lineage tracking.

title: task: refactor localizedContent.ts index-coupling to use data-driven mapping
description:
src/i18n/localizedContent.ts (250 lines) uses a brittle index-to-key coupling to link siteConfig arrays to translation dictionary keys. For example, getLocalizedExperience() assumes siteConfig.experience[0] is Crosstide, [1] is Pret, etc., and adds hardcoded spread conditionals for each index. Adding a 7th job means touching both siteConfig AND the function body AND all four dictionaries AND the guard assertions at the top.

There are also four console.assert() calls that fire in production builds (lines 17-34, guarded only in NODE_ENV === 'test'), which is unconventional and contributes noise in dev builds.

Suggested refactor:
1. Replace the index-based coupling with an explicit map: siteConfig entries get a stable string key (e.g. company identifier), and the dict keys match by that identifier. Removes the spread-conditional tower.
2. Move the structural invariant checks to a build-time type assertion or a dedicated test, not a module-level console.assert.

This is a refactoring task with no user-visible impact but significant maintenance payoff when experience entries change. Assign to senior-frontend-engineer.
close_reason: Completed: localized project and experience strings now map by stable contentKey values instead of array position; module-load console.assert guards were replaced by regression tests.

title: Segment dev key placeholder causes 404 in local gatsby develop
description: gatsby-plugin-segment-js uses devKey='SEGMENT_DEV_WRITE_KEY' (placeholder), so cdn.segment.io returns 404 in local 'gatsby develop'. This trips header-navigation.spec.ts ':194 free of runtime errors' (console 404) locally only. CI uses 'npm run serve' with the real prodKey, so it passes there. Pre-existing, unrelated to i18n. Fix: gate Segment loading in dev or set a valid devKey.
close_reason: Fixed: removed the placeholder Segment devKey so gatsby develop no longer requests cdn.segment.io with SEGMENT_DEV_WRITE_KEY; added config regression coverage.

title: Layout.tsx: remove redundant PropTypes import — TypeScript interface already enforces types
description: Layout.tsx simultaneously uses a TypeScript LayoutProps interface AND attaches a PropTypes runtime check (Layout.propTypes = { children: PropTypes.node.isRequired }). Since react/prop-types is disabled in eslint.config.mjs for .ts/.tsx files and TypeScript already enforces the interface, the PropTypes check is dead code. Fix: remove the 'import PropTypes from prop-types' line and the Layout.propTypes assignment. Assign to: senior-frontend-engineer.
close_reason: Removed PropTypes import and Layout.propTypes assignment from Layout.tsx; TypeScript interface already enforces the children prop type

title: Clean up remaining React namespace imports in Head.tsx, Layout.tsx, ThemeToggle.tsx
description: After the React.FC removal refactor, three files still use the React default import as a namespace: Head.tsx uses React.useEffect (should be named import), Layout.tsx uses React.ReactNode in props interface (should be import type { ReactNode }), ThemeToggle.tsx imports React but never uses it as a namespace. These were out of scope for the original remove-react-fc task (dlo-7q9) but represent the same class of issue. Fix: convert to named imports and remove the default React import from all three files.

This is now unnecessary under the automatic JSX runtime. Follow-on cleanup pass to remove these stale imports from all test files to be consistent with the production source files refactored in dlo-7q9.
